title: [February 26, 2026] Proposed Changes to Spark for Upcoming Spell
post1Raw:
## Summary

1. [Ethereum] Spark Treasury - Spark Foundation Grant for March (Exec)
2. [Ethereum] Spark Treasury - SPK Buybacks Transfer for February 2026 (Exec)
3. [Ethereum] SparkLend - Claim Reserves (Exec)
4. [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Increase Rate Limit for SparkLend USDT
5. [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Increase Rate Limit for Maple syrupUSDT
6. [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Increase Rate Limit for Aave Core USDT
7. [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Onboard with Paxos (Exec)
8. [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Onboard Morpho v2 USDT Vault

## Rationale

### [Ethereum] Spark Treasury - Spark Foundation Grant for March (Exec)

This is a recurring item that can go directly to executive vote based on approval in the Atlas. This transfer will provide funding to Spark Foundation for the month of March 2026.

**Parameter summary:**

* Transfer 1.1 million USDS to Spark Foundation at 0x92e4629a4510AF5819d7D1601464C233599fF5ec

### [Ethereum] Spark Treasury - SPK Buybacks Transfer for February 2026 (Exec)

This is a recurring item that can go directly to executive vote based on approval in the Atlas. Spark previously adopted a SubDAO Proxy Management plan, where excess value within the SubDAO Proxy is periodically transferred to an executor to be used to buy back SPK. Based on the current value held in the Spark SubDAO Proxy and relevant target treasury metrics, the calculated buyback amount for February 2026 is 571,957 USDS. This will be transferred to the designated buyback executor, the Spark Operations Multisig, to be used to purchase SPK. The received SPK will then be transferred to the buyback recipient, which is set as the Spark SubDAO Proxy.

Details of buyback calculations will be listed in a separate [thread](https://forum.skyeco.com/t/spark-subdao-proxy-management-updates/27734).

**Parameter summary:**

* Transfer 571,957 USDS to the Spark Operations Multisig at 0x2E1b01adABB8D4981863394bEa23a1263CBaeDfC

### [Ethereum] SparkLend - Claim Reserves (Exec)

This is a recurring item that can go directly to executive vote based on approval in the Atlas. Claiming and consolidating SparkLend reserves increases Spark’s available risk capital, allowing for more revenue generating capacity for the SLL.

**Parameter summary:**

* Claim all reserves
* Transfer non-USD stablecoin reserves to Spark Operations Multisig 0x2E1b01adABB8D4981863394bEa23a1263CBaeDfC to be liquidated

### [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Increase Rate Limit for SparkLend USDT

Spark is planning to significantly scale up the Spark Savings spUSDT vault, and we anticipate the existing SLL rate limits for USDT allocations may be insufficient to support the level of deposit and withdrawal flows going forward, resulting in carrying unallocated USDT at a loss. We propose to increase the rate limits for SparkLend, Maple, and Aave USDT allocations to match expected upper bound usage rates, while continuing to maintain appropriate protections against faults in the ALM planner and other SLL components.

SparkLend is fully governed under Spark and Sky, so there is minimal risk of allocating capital here or allowing for permissive rate limits. However, we will continue to implement risk mitigation features into both SparkLend and SLL (at both the onchain ALM controller level, as well as offchain ALM planner level) to further reduce tail risk exposure to the protocol.

**Parameter summary:**

* SLL configuration for SparkLend USDT
  * Deposits
    * maxAmount: 250 million
    * slope: 2 billion per day
  * Withdrawals
    * maxAmount: Unlimited

### [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Increase Rate Limit for Aave Core USDT

The overall reasoning for increasing rate limits is the same as above. Specifically for Aave Core USDT, because the market is not under the Spark governance umbrella we propose to set more restrictive rate limits, and particularly a lower maxAmount to give Spark and external teams more time to react to any possible faults before a significant amount of capital is deployed.

We propose to set a 10 million maximum amount for a single deposit to Aave USDT in the onchain level, which will ensure that any single deposit would be manageable compared to Spark’s risk capital, and give time to halt new deposits if occurring during a tail risk situation on Aave. Spark also plans to implement further safeguards at the level of the ALM Planner (offchain allocation logic) to mitigate risk and limit deposits when risk conditions are elevated.

In tandem with the conservative max amount parameter, we propose a relatively high slope (daily throughput) to enable SLL to appropriately rebalance capital between allocations even if Spark Savings USDT experiences significant deposit or withdrawal flows, or if Aave sees large rate volatility. Historically we see semi-regular USDT deposits and withdrawals at Aave of hundreds of millions to even above 1 billion USDT, so it is evident that this sort of large scale Tether activity should be expected going forward. The combination of low max amount and large slope strikes the optimal balance for Spark of limiting exposure to Aave technical or bad debt risks, while also minimizing Spark’s financial risks and opportunity costs that can arise from being unable to rebalance liquidity quickly enough.

**Parameter summary:**

* SLL configuration for Aave Core USDT
  * Deposits
    * maxAmount: 10 million
    * slope: 1 billion per day
  * Withdrawals
    * maxAmount: Unlimited

### [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Increase Rate Limit for Maple syrupUSDT

The overall reasoning for increasing rate limits is the same as above. Because Maple allocates to term loans and has relatively lower liquidity, SLL is expected to use it as a smaller, more tactical allocation venue for USDT to improve yield while keeping overall exposure and liquidity risk down. For this reason, we don’t expect to need as high of rate limits as for SparkLend or Aave noted above (which are the primary venues for supplying USDT liquidity backing Spark Savings).

Unlike most other SLL allocations, the withdrawal rate limits are not set as unlimited. Because Maple redemptions may not be atomic depending on available liquidity, we believe it is appropriate to limit maximum withdrawal throughputs to avoid a situation where an SLL fault creates a large volume of pending withdrawals. The proposed withdrawal rate limits should be more that sufficient to clear all syrupUSDT within a day or less under expected allocation volumes, so this should not materially impact SLL’s ability to meet USDT redemptions or rebalance liquidity.

**Parameter summary:**

* SLL configuration for Maple syrupUSDT
  * Deposits
    * maxAmount: 25 million
    * slope: 100 million
  * Request Redemption
    * maxAmount: 50 million
    * slope: 500 million
  * Withdrawals
    * maxAmount: unlimited

### [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Onboard with Paxos (Exec)

This item can move directly to executive vote, based on a prior SPK poll approval of this item (link [here](https://snapshot.org/#/s:sparkfi.eth/proposal/0xf1a95e7ddaf2f95008608d7e27d8bed9ba6e9c7c55060e8e595f414d88e6b5c9)). Onboarding with Paxos will allow Spark to access mint/redeem capabilities, improving swap efficiency between USDC, PYUSD, and USDG. In the future, this account access may also be used for bridging Paxos stablecoins between various chains where they are natively supported.

**Parameter summary:**

* USDC transferAsset (sell USDC to buy PYUSD)
  * maxAmount: 5 million
  * slope: 50 million
  * address: 0xFb1F749024b4544c425f5CAf6641959da31EdF37
* PYUSD transferAsset (sell PYUSD to buy USDC)
  * maxAmount: 5 million
  * slope: 200 million
  * address: 0x2f7BE67e11A4D621E36f1A8371b0a5Fe16dE6B20
* PYUSD transferAsset (sell PYUSD to buy USDG)
  * maxAmount: 5 million
  * slope: 50 million
  * address: 0x227B1912C2fFE1353EA3A603F1C05F030Cc262Ff
* USDG transferAsset (sell USDG to buy PYUSD)
  * maxAmount: 5 million
  * slope: 100 million
  * address: 0x035b322D0e79de7c8733CdDA5a7EF8b51a6cfcfa

### [Ethereum] Spark Liquidity Layer - Onboard Morpho v2 USDT Vault

Onboarding a Morpho v2 USDT vault will give SLL an additional venue for allocating USDT liquidity. This is particularly valuable in the short term as it will allow Spark to lend USDT against RWAs, tokenized strategy vaults, and other assets that are more appropriate for isolated collateral mechanisms rather than pooled collateral/cross margin like SparkLend.

The Morpho vault will adhere to our standard permissions and admin structure, with allocator role held by SLL, curator held by a Spark multisig, Sentinel (Guardian / veto role) held by external oversight bodies affiliated with Sky, and owner held by governance (the Spark SubDAO Proxy). Becuase the vault will operate under the same Spark and Sky governance root authority as SparkLend, it is safe to implement relatively high SLL rate limits, which will give the SLL better flexibility to accommodate potential high deposit and withdrawal flows in the Spark Savings USDT vault.

**Parameter summary:**

* Morpho vault configuration
  * Address: 0xc7CDcFDEfC64631ED6799C95e3b110cd42F2bD22
  * Allocator: 0x9Ad87668d49ab69EEa0AF091de970EF52b0D5178 (ALM Proxy Freezable)
  * Curator: 0x0f963A8A8c01042B69054e787E5763ABbB0646A3
  * Sentinel (Guardian): 0xf5748bBeFa17505b2F7222B23ae11584932C908B
  * Owner: 0x3300f198988e4C9C63F75dF86De36421f06af8c4 (SubDAO Proxy)
* SLL configuration
  * Deposits
    * maxAmount: 50 million
    * slope: 1 billion
  * Withdrawals
    * maxAmount: Unlimited

**Update (17 Feb 2026):** Added transferAsset addresses for Paxos onboarding.

**Update (18 Feb 2026):** Clarified that rate limit for Maple withdrawals is for requesting a redemption. Claiming the withdrawal is unlimited.

**Update (19 Feb 2026):** Added deployed Morpho USDT vault address, clarified admin is ALM Proxy *Freezable* as per standard operating procedure for Morpho vault roles, and made text correction in Paxos onboarding item
